title: jarava psylantha
intro: Jarava psyllantha, also known as Queñua or Inkari, is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Jarava psyllantha include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to make poultices and creams for wound healing.
